title.en: Chinese New Year and Rosh Hashana: Two New Year Traditions Compared
title.zh: 中国新年与犹太新年:两种新年传统的比较
title.he: ראש השנה הסיני וראש השנה היהודי: השוואה בין שתי מסורות השנה החדשה

description.en: Family meals, symbolic foods, new beginnings, honoring ancestors — how these two new year celebrations share surprising parallels.
description.zh: 家庭聚餐、象征性食物、新的开始、尊崇祖先——这两个新年庆祝活动如何分享令人惊讶的相似之处。
description.he: ארוחות משפחתיות, מאכלים סמליים, התחלות חדשות, כיבוד אבות - כיצד שתי חגיגות השנה החדשה הללו חולקות קווי דמיון מפתיעים.

author: Torah Light AI
keyThemes: Symbolic foods and their meanings in both traditions
Family reunion as the heart of both celebrations
Physical and spiritual preparation for renewal
Sound as marker of transition (shofar and firecrackers)
Reflection, judgment, and hope for the new year
Universal human needs expressed through particular cultural forms
notes: This article focuses specifically on the PARALLELS between Chinese New Year and Rosh Hashana, emphasizing shared rituals, symbolic foods, family gatherings, and themes of renewal. It differs from article #401 (Three New Years) which explored why different cultures celebrate new years at different times. This article is less about calendrical differences and more about the remarkable similarities in how these two ancient traditions approach the concept of renewal, family connection, and spiritual reflection.
